meta_desc: What Hallucine’s customers say: installations on historic monuments, quick set-ups, ultra-lightweight and durable screens.
hero_desc: From the Palais des Papes to the Stade Vélodrome in Marseille, event organisers share their experiences of using Hallucine inflatable screens.
intro: Impossible installations made possible, lightning-fast set-ups, screens that can withstand anything: here, in their own words, is what our customers have to say.
signature_title: The projects that showcase our expertise
signature_desc: When a client needs a projection that seems impossible — a restricted site, extreme constraints, or an extraordinary scale — we find a solution. Here are the stories that prove it.
signature_velodrome_teaser: A 24-metre screen installed on the pitch at the Stade Orange Vélodrome in Marseille, without any lifting equipment and without leaving a single mark.
signature_oran_teaser: A 12-metre-high screen on Oran’s main square: the organiser called on the army to take down the lanterns that were hanging too low.
photo_alt: Open-air cinema screening on an inflatable screen: ‘Hallucine’ at sunset
t1_quote: My 15-year-old son, with no experience whatsoever, put up our 10-metre screen all by himself in 45 minutes.
t2_quote: When I received my 10-metre screen, the parcel was so small that I rang Hallucine to check there hadn’t been a mistake. It’s much more compact, lighter and sturdier than my old screens.
t3_quote: One of our screens with a continuous air flow was slashed twice with a knife. Nevertheless, I was able to continue my three-month screening without any problems.
t4_quote: I was asked to move the screen less than an hour before the session. As it’s so lightweight, I moved it and put it back in place without the slightest difficulty.
t5_quote: We mounted the screen on the façade of the Palais des Papes, a historic monument where traditional mounting methods were prohibited. Hallucine’s lightweight and versatile screen made this possible.
t6_quote: We had to install a screen on the roof of the Quai Branly Museum, navigating narrow tunnels and hatches. With no lifting equipment available, it was only the Hallucine screen that made this installation possible.
t7_quote: We installed a 24-metre screen at the Stade Vélodrome in Marseille without damaging the pitch — moving it by hand, without any machinery. The requirements were very strict; only a Hallucine screen could meet them.
t8_quote: We were able to mount the screen on a pole at our theatre without worrying about breaking the poles or the ropes.
cta_desc: Tell us about your project: we’ll recommend the right screen for your requirements.
